item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I quite like this one! Relatively simple and and the way the box changes its actions based on the user's interpretation of "inside out" is a neat addition.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: probably dumb question: what would happen if a solid object consisting of a single material was placed inside? im thinking it would (if possible) fill the box except for an air bubble in the original item in the center

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The only criticism (if you can call it that) I have is why the foundation was alerted to the box after what could be seen as the fault of the company, seems a bit quick, but other than that extremely well written!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My guess? "Outside". In the most literal sense of the word, it turns things inside out so "Inside" literally becomes "Outside". If you're asking what would happen to SCP-2719, it's not really a thing that can be turned inside out as it's a metaphysical pointer. Turning concepts inside out would be trippy as heck.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I second this. This was well written.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love it. Only problem I have: it still has the default [ACCESS DENIED] as its name on the series 4 list.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You know the rule with cats and boxes…

If it fits, it sits.
If it's inside out, give a shout.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Cats in boxes? Get outta here Schrodinger. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The comment on squeamishness wasn't intended to be a jab at the D-class, more so a note on their file so that people choosing them for other experiments would know that they might not be the best fit for certain things.

I have added a mild telling off by a senior research officer in that area though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Somehow I knew this was going to end with a cat being tested… poor kitty. But great article! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nice clean, simple scip. I dig it. What would happen if it was cross-tested with SCP-2719 I wonder…

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
D-0192 expressed a desire to insert a member of research personnel into SCP-3652 and as such was reassigned,

I was prolly gonna upvote anyway, but this bit of flavor secured it.

Also, nice that the last test defied expectations, but in a non-dramatic way. Like, it didn't write "nice try" on the shirt or anything. Still just a box :)

Edit: since it was strange to me that anybody, regardless of education, would have a "default" definition of inside out , maybe make it "most comprehensive definition of inside out"? Otherwise, I'm choosing to believe the box was just created to blow up physicists.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I personally love these. Also, this is the sort of thing that really calls for a collab testing log.

Item: Dr. Harris's antique nickel-plated wristwatch
Subject: Researcher Jobes, 29 years of age
Procedure: Subject placed item inside box and then closed it.
Results: Small metal and scraping sounds observed for 2-3 minutes after box was closed. Dr. Harris discovered his watch was missing during the testing procedure and attempted unsuccessfully to gain entry to the box for the remainder of the 5 minute period. Upon recovery and examination, the movement gears and other previously internal workings of the watch were now situated outside the case, while the leather of the strap was reshaped and now contained inside the case. Additionally, the watch no longer functioned.
Analysis: Researcher Jobes has been placed on probation (again).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
“Squeamish” added to D-0892’s file, to be reviewed as their reaction is odd considering the acts committed which led to them being acquired by the Foundation as D-class personnel.

I don't really like this. Any instance of Foundation researchers taking shots at D-Class personnel for what they did prior to becoming D-Class, even vague and offhanded instances like this, just rub me the wrong way. It just seems out of place and a bit unprofessional to me.

That's a minor SCPeeve, though. Overall, it's not bad, but it's not particular gripping for me. It has a curious effect that's thoroughly explored, and while I admittedly have a soft spot somewhere for things that do a weird thing with a test log demonstrating said weirdness, nowadays, that soft spot doesn't really do all that much for me unless what it does is really mind-blowing, or there's some kind of other hook that leads into something deeper.

For what it is, though, I think it's alright. Novote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it! The writing style is on point, very clean and concise.
The testing logs was actually useful and interesting, as it shows how the Foundation would progress from testing to testing, and not just"**this is what happens with this! And now this!", but instead a logical "Let's try this. Is it the only way? Let's try it again. Now with different secondary terms." Quite nice

+1

Also, I enjoy the lack of expungements. Good work, buddy

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah you're right, there's really no good reason to withhold the site. Changed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Other than this:

Site-███

I quite like this SCP.

+1
